stem: Priya earns $\$780$ per week. Calculate her gross annual income.
hint: There are $52$ weeks in a year. Multiply the weekly wage by $52$.

stem: Liam earns $\$2340$ per fortnight. Calculate his gross annual income.
hint: There are $26$ fortnights in a year. Multiply the fortnightly wage by $26$.

stem: Mei earns $\$1125$ per week. Calculate her gross annual income.
Daniel earns $\$2860$ per fortnight. Calculate his gross annual income.

James has a taxable income of $\$32\,000$ per year. Using the tax table below, calculate his annual income tax.

| Taxable income | Tax payable |
|:---|:---|
| $\$0 - \$18\,200$ | Nil |
| $\$18\,201 - \$45\,000$ | $19$ c for each $\$1$ over $\$18\,200$ |
hint: Subtract $\$18\,200$ from the taxable income to find the amount in the second bracket, then multiply by $0.19$.

stem:
Sarah has a taxable income of $\$56\,000$ per year. Using the tax table below, calculate her annual income tax.

| Taxable income | Tax payable |
|:---|:---|
| $\$0 - \$18\,200$ | Nil |
| $\$18\,201 - \$45\,000$ | $19$ c for each $\$1$ over $\$18\,200$ |
| $\$45\,001 - \$120\,000$ | $\$5092$ plus $32.5$ c for each $\$1$ over $\$45\,000$ |
hint: The income falls in the third bracket. Use the base tax of $\$5092$ and add $32.5$ c for each dollar above $\$45\,000$.

stem:
Chen has a taxable income of $\$27\,000$ per year. Using the tax table below, calculate his annual income tax.

| Taxable income | Tax payable |
|:---|:---|
| $\$0 - \$18\,200$ | Nil |
| $\$18\,201 - \$45\,000$ | $19$ c for each $\$1$ over $\$18\,200$ |
hint: Find how much of the income falls above $\$18\,200$, then multiply by $0.19$.

stem:
Aisha has a taxable income of $\$78\,600$ per year. Using the tax table below, calculate her annual income tax.

| Taxable income | Tax payable |
|:---|:---|
| $\$0 - \$18\,200$ | Nil |
| $\$18\,201 - \$45\,000$ | $19$ c for each $\$1$ over $\$18\,200$ |
| $\$45\,001 - \$120\,000$ | $\$5092$ plus $32.5$ c for each $\$1$ over $\$45\,000$ |
hint: The income falls in the third bracket. Start with $\$5092$ and add $32.5$ c for each dollar above $\$45\,000$.

stem:
Madison earns $\$800$ per week. She has $\$1000$ in allowable deductions. Using the tax table below, calculate her net weekly pay after income tax and deductions. Give your answer to the nearest cent.

| Taxable income | Tax payable |
|:---|:---|
| $\$0 - \$18\,200$ | Nil |
| $\$18\,201 - \$45\,000$ | $19$ c for each $\$1$ over $\$18\,200$ |
hint: First find gross annual income ($\times 52$), then calculate tax, subtract deductions and tax to get net annual income, then divide by $52$.

stem:
Tom earns $\$1050$ per week. He has $\$1200$ in allowable deductions. Using the tax table below, calculate his net weekly pay after income tax and deductions.

| Taxable income | Tax payable |
|:---|:---|
| $\$0 - \$18\,200$ | Nil |
| $\$18\,201 - \$45\,000$ | $19$ c for each $\$1$ over $\$18\,200$ |
| $\$45\,001 - \$120\,000$ | $\$5092$ plus $32.5$ c for each $\$1$ over $\$45\,000$ |
hint: Find gross annual income, identify the correct tax bracket, calculate tax, then find net annual and net weekly income.

stem:
Grace earns $\$3200$ per fortnight. She has $\$2500$ in allowable deductions. Using the tax table below, calculate her net weekly pay after income tax and deductions. Give your answer to the nearest cent.

| Taxable income | Tax payable |
|:---|:---|
| $\$0 - \$18\,200$ | Nil |
| $\$18\,201 - \$45\,000$ | $19$ c for each $\$1$ over $\$18\,200$ |
| $\$45\,001 - \$120\,000$ | $\$5092$ plus $32.5$ c for each $\$1$ over $\$45\,000$ |
hint: Convert fortnightly wage to annual ($\times 26$), find the correct tax bracket, calculate tax, then find net weekly pay.

stem:
Marcus earns $\$2800$ per fortnight. He has $\$1800$ in allowable deductions for work-related expenses and union fees. Using the tax table below, calculate his net weekly pay after income tax and deductions. Give your answer to the nearest cent.

| Taxable income | Tax payable |
|:---|:---|
| $\$0 - \$18\,200$ | Nil |
| $\$18\,201 - \$45\,000$ | $19$ c for each $\$1$ over $\$18\,200$ |
| $\$45\,001 - \$120\,000$ | $\$5092$ plus $32.5$ c for each $\$1$ over $\$45\,000$ |
hint: Convert fortnightly wage to annual ($\times 26$), calculate tax from the table, then find net weekly income.
